name: black-elk-speaks
description: The spiritual autobiography of Black Elk, a Lakota holy man — understanding vision, the sacred hoop, ceremony, and resilience in the face of cultural destruction.
useCase: Vision integration, seeking spiritual connection to nature, maintaining faith through destruction, incorporating ceremony into daily life, bearing witness to truth.
knownRisks: Black Elk's words were recorded and shaped by John Neihardt — a non-Native poet
The book has a complex relationship with Lakota tradition — some scholars critique Neihardt's literary intrusions
This is a spiritual text, not a therapeutic or academic guide
